jei.alone.waterskin.desc: Right-click a water source to fill it (4 draughts); hold right-click to drink. Raw water risks sickness — swamp/jungle water is worse. A skin can't go over a fire, so boil water clean in a fire-safe pot and pour it in, or fill from clean rain or a water cauldron.
jei.alone.clay_pot.desc: A fire-safe vessel AND a rain-catcher. Right-click the ground with it empty to set it down; under open sky it slowly fills with CLEAN rainwater (right-click the placed pot to drink, or right-click it with a waterskin/pot to fill that vessel). Held, right-click water to fill and hold right-click to drink; being fire-safe, set it over a campfire to boil murky water clean. A tarp pitched 1-4 blocks above funnels rain into pots below.
jei.alone.iron_pot.desc: The big fire-safe vessel — holds far more water than a clay pot and works the same. Right-click the ground with it empty to set it down as a rain-catcher (fills clean under open sky; right-click to drink or to fill another vessel). Held, right-click water to fill, hold right-click to drink, and set it over a fire to boil water clean.
jei.alone.hot_rock.desc: Boils water when you have no fire-safe pot. First heat it: sneak + right-click a LIT campfire while holding a plain Rock. Then, with the hot rock in your main hand and a NON-fire-safe vessel (a waterskin) holding water in your off hand, sneak + right-click the air to drop it in and boil the water CLEAN. It cools as you carry it (the durability bar is its heat) and reverts to a plain Rock; gone too cool, it won't boil.
jei.alone.bow_drill.desc: A friction fire-starter. Hold it and hold right-click on an UNLIT campfire to drill (keep the cursor on it); after enough strokes it catches. Far faster and less tiring than a bare stick, but like all friction fire it FAILS in rain or when you're wet — only a ferro rod lights in the wet. It wears out and eventually needs re-stringing.
jei.alone.ferro_rod.desc: The fast, reliable fire steel. Hold it and hold right-click on an UNLIT campfire to shower sparks; it catches in just a few strokes for almost no effort — and it's the ONLY fire-starter that still works in the rain or when you're wet. Wears down slowly over many strikes.
jei.alone.flint_and_pyrite.desc: A strike-a-light. Hold it and hold right-click on an UNLIT campfire to strike it alight — quick and near-effortless, but the spark needs dry tinder, so it fails in the rain (a bow drill also fails wet; only a forged ferro rod lights in the wet). Wears out after ~40 fires.
jei.alone.ember.desc: Carry fire between camps. Sneak + right-click a LIT campfire with an empty hand to scoop a glowing ember (it costs the fire some fuel). Then right-click an UNLIT campfire while holding the ember to light it instantly — no drilling. It cools as you carry it (the durability bar is its life) and dies to cold charcoal if you don't use it in time.
jei.alone.torch.desc: Crafted UNLIT — you must light it before it gives light. Right-click a fire source (campfire, fire, lava, a lit torch) to light it, or hold flint and steel in your other hand and right-click. Once lit it burns down over time (the durability bar is its remaining fuel); place a lit torch to set an ordinary torch block.
jei.alone.rock.desc: A do-everything stone. Plain right-click THROWS it at small game (a weak, arcing shot). Hold Flint in one hand and a Rock in the other, then hold right-click to KNAP a flint shard. Sneak + right-click a lit campfire to heat it into a Hot Rock for boiling. It's also the ammo a slingshot fires.
jei.alone.flint_knife.desc: A cutting blade. Kill a hide-bearing animal while holding a blade (this, a sword, axe, or spear) to butcher the carcass for raw hide, bone, and sinew — a fists/pick kill wastes them. Hold right-click on grass/ferns/vines to strip 2-3 plant fibre at once (bare hands: 1-2).
jei.alone.flint_hatchet.desc: Chops wood like a vanilla axe, but also RIVES planks by hand: hold a log in one hand and the hatchet in the other, then sneak + hold right-click to split the log into ~2 planks over ~14s (costs stamina). A metal axe (3 planks) or a hand saw (4) does it faster for less sweat.
jei.alone.hand_saw.desc: Rives planks far faster than splitting by hand: hold a log in one hand and the saw in the other, then sneak + hold right-click to saw it into 4 clean boards (~6s, low stamina). The woodworking upgrade over a flint hatchet or bare axe.
jei.alone.whetstone.desc: Hones a worn edge back up. Hold the damaged tool or weapon in your MAIN hand and the whetstone in your OFF hand, then sneak + right-click to restore ~25% of its durability. The whetstone itself wears with use. It won't sharpen armour.
jei.alone.kiln.desc: Fires pottery, with no menu — you use it by right-clicking. Right-click with unfired ware (an unfired clay pot or a clay ball) to load it, then right-click again with any campfire fuel to feed it; it fires on its own (~2 min) as long as it has fuel. Right-click empty-handed to take out the finished piece.
jei.alone.bloomery.desc: Smelts iron, with no menu. Right-click with raw iron to load it, then right-click with CHARCOAL or coal (a plain wood fire isn't hot enough) to fuel it; it smelts on its own (~3 min). Right-click empty-handed to pull out the iron bloom — a spongy lump you still have to hammer into an ingot.
jei.alone.smithing_hammer.desc: Forges metal gear. Iron and steel tools/armour come off the crafting grid as BRITTLE, near-useless blanks — you must forge them: hold the blank in your main hand near a lit fire or forge to heat it (watch the action bar), then, with a smithing hammer anywhere in your pack, right-click an ANVIL to strike a blow. It takes several blows over multiple reheats to finish and set the piece's quality. The hammer wears down with use.
jei.alone.splint.desc: Treats a sprained joint. Once you've sprained an ankle (e.g. from a bad fall or overloaded jump), hold a splint and sneak + right-click to bind it, mending the sprain about 4x faster. Consumes one splint.
jei.alone.herbal_remedy.desc: Medicine, not food — hold right-click to drink it. It settles a foodborne illness, dysentery, and a festering infection, and clears nausea and poison. The treatment the sickness conditions were missing.
jei.alone.sewing_kit.desc: Mends worn leather/hide clothing by hand. Wear the damaged leather armour, hold the kit, and hold right-click to patch the most-worn piece (~35% durability back). Each patch spends one Plant Fibre as thread, plus a little of the kit. It won't repair metal armour.
jei.alone.towel.desc: Right-click to instantly rub yourself dry (clears wetness, which is what drives wet-cold). It goes damp after one use and can't be used again until it dries out (~60s) — and it re-wets at once if you're still standing in rain or water.
jei.alone.backpack.desc: Portable 27-slot storage — its contents travel inside the item. Right-click to open it. Sneak + right-click a surface to set it down as a block; right-click the block to open it, and break it to pick the pack back up with everything still inside.
jei.alone.woven_basket.desc: A back-slung carry aid that raises how much VOLUME you can carry — just keep one anywhere in your inventory and the bonus applies (the basket itself is excused from counting against your load). Extra baskets don't stack the bonus and count as ordinary cargo.
jei.alone.travois.desc: A dragged cargo sled for hauling loads overland. Right-click the ground to set it down (it faces the way you're looking); hit it to pack it back into this item.
jei.alone.rope.desc: A climbing line. Right-click a block face to anchor a rope that hangs one block down; right-click the TOP of a hanging line to pay out another length down a cliff. Climb it up and down freely (crouch to descend), like a ladder. Break the top to reel the whole line back into your pack.
jei.alone.slingshot.desc: A ranged weapon for small game. Carry loose Rocks as ammo. Hold right-click to draw (a longer draw = more power and range), release to loose a pebble at what you're aiming at. Each shot spends one rock. Drops rabbits, birds, squirrels; barely dents big animals.
jei.alone.wicker_shield.desc: A primitive shield. Hold right-click to raise it and block attacks like any shield. It has the lowest durability of the shields, and a large creature (bear, bison, boar, ravager, golem, warden) bashes it aside for LONGER than a sturdier wooden or metal shield.
jei.alone.wooden_shield.desc: Hold right-click to raise it and block attacks like any shield. Sturdier than a wicker shield (more durability) and harder to knock aside — but a big creature (bear, bison, boar, ravager, golem, warden) can still bash your guard down and stagger you; it just recovers faster than wicker would.
jei.alone.bug_net.desc: Right-click to wear it on your head. While worn, biting insects near standing water can't reach you — no stamina drain, no wetland fever. It occupies your HELMET slot (you can't also wear a helmet). A smudge fire (a lit campfire) does the same for anyone nearby.
jei.alone.snare.desc: A passive small-game trap. Place it on solid ground and leave it — over time it catches a rabbit on its own (drawing down the local game). Right-click a sprung snare to collect the catch (sometimes a hide too); it then resets and keeps working. No bait needed.
jei.alone.deadfall.desc: A baited trap with better odds than a snare. Place it on solid ground, then right-click it with any FOOD to arm it. Left alone it catches 1-2 rabbits (plus hides); right-click to collect — but it collapses and must be RE-BAITED each time.
jei.alone.fish_trap.desc: A passive fish weir. Place it on the water surface (any shore or pond works) and leave it; over time it catches a fish on its own. Right-click to take the catch, then it resets. No bait and no rod needed.
jei.alone.gill_net.desc: A passive fishing net for OPEN, deep water only (it won't fish a shore or a puddle). Place it on the surface; it catches faster than a weir and holds a batch of up to 3 fish. Right-click to haul up the whole batch at once.
jei.alone.worms.desc: Fishing bait. Carry them while you fish with a rod and each catch spends one worm to land the fish AND tempt up a bonus second fish, even from a spot that's been fished thin. With a bare hook (no worms), most catches slip away.
jei.alone.drying_rack.desc: Right-click with perishable food to hang it and air-dry it into non-spoiling jerky (hot/dry weather is faster; a lit campfire two blocks below with an air gap smokes it fastest — but a fire directly beneath burns the rack down, and warm+wet weather rots the food instead). Right-click with a raw hide to brain-tan it into leather (needs Animal Brains in your inventory). Right-click to check progress; sneak + right-click to pull an item off early.
jei.alone.raw_hide.desc: The green skin off a fresh kill — NOT leather yet, and it has no use except to be tanned. Hang it on a drying rack (you'll need Animal Brains on you to tan it) and leave it days to turn it into real leather. A bone scraper carried in your pack halves the tanning time.
jei.alone.animal_brains.desc: The brain-tanning agent — every kill yields about enough to tan its own hide. Its only use: keep it in your inventory when you hang a Raw Hide on a drying rack, and it's consumed to tan the hide into leather.
jei.alone.bone_scraper.desc: Speeds hide tanning — just carry it. When you hang a raw hide on a drying rack, the scraper fleshes it on the spot so it tans in HALF the time. The scraping wears the tool down. Tanning still works without one, just slower.
jei.alone.thatch.desc: A primitive roof. Place it overhead and it blocks the sky, so the space beneath counts as SHELTER — being roofed keeps the rain off (you stay dry) and holds warmth at night. The day-one roof, long before you can saw planks. Warning: it's bone-dry straw and burns readily, so keep it well clear of your fire.
jei.alone.tarp.desc: The premium roof: like thatch it shelters the space beneath (roofed, dry, warmer), but it's FIREPROOF — pitch it right over a campfire to keep flame and rain off at once — and reusable (break it and carry it on). Pitched 1-4 blocks above clay/iron pots, it also funnels rain down to fill them faster.
jei.alone.wattle.desc: A woven wall panel. Walls, not just a roof, are what make a shelter hold its warmth — enclose a roofed space with wattle and it gets far snugger (each of the four sides that's walled helps). Cheap and renewable, but flammable and weak: the day-one wall, below log and stone.
jei.alone.bedroll.desc: A portable bed: place it and right-click to sleep at night and set your spawn, like a vanilla bed. It also lets you lie down and REST during the day to pass hours and shed fatigue. It gives no extra warmth, though — on a cold night a sleeping bag rests you better.
jei.alone.sleeping_bag.desc: A warmth-rated bed: place and sleep like a bedroll, but while you're in it it adds strong insulation, so a cold or even freezing night still rests you fully (a bedroll leaves you shivering). It cuts both ways, though — on a WARM night it's a sweatbox that ruins your sleep, and a soaked bag holds almost no heat, so keep it dry and save it for the cold.
